caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the work done per unit mass of gas during compression processes. The slide titled "Tutorial - 3" includes a detailed thermodynamic diagram illustrating various processes such as isothermal (T=const), isobaric (p=const), and isenthalpic (h=const) transformations. A formula is presented: \(-\frac{W_c}{m} = T_1(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)\), which calculates the work done per unit mass of gas compressed. The cursor moves over the formula, likely highlighting or explaining its components. The presenter, Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, uses this visual aid to explain the principles of thermodynamics in cryogenic systems, emphasizing the relationships between temperature, pressure, entropy, and enthalpy during different stages of gas compression.
